mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns because rising employment expands household income and consumption, lifting corporate earnings expectations. This relationship holds continuously without threshold conditioning.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive monthly PAYEMS changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL level above 240 signals elevated inflation that compresses equity valuations through expected monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 20-day returns. The signal activates when the price index crosses this threshold.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 240 coinciding with rising inflation are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad demand momentum that exceeds single-sector effects, driving positive S&P 500 60-day returns through aggregate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high, monthly decreases in UNRATE signal economic healing that boosts consumption expectations and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 20-day returns. This mechanism is inactive when joblessness is low.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE exceeds 5%, monthly decreases in UNRATE do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises in industrial production growth (INDPRO month-over-month > 0.5%) indicate accelerating capacity utilization that flows immediately into manufacturing sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 5-day returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5% is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) increases the opportunity cost of holding equities and raises discount rates applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 60-day returns through sustained earnings yield re-rating.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% fail to produce negative 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) drives S&P 500 60-day returns by altering aggregate demand expectations, but this effect is only reliable when concurrent payroll growth (PAYEMS) confirms the demand is labor-market-backed, avoiding jobless-recovery false positives.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and PAYEMS fails to achieve Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year window, or GDPC1 alone outperforms the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in UNRATE (> 0.2pp) triggers systematic risk-off deleveraging as algorithms and investors revise growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month UNRATE increases greater than 0.2pp are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered months.
